easy.title: Create a 'CSS Color Reference' Page
easy.description: Build a color reference page that demonstrates all five color methods (named, hex, RGB, RGBA, HSL). Create a table with 10+ colors, each showing the color preview, the color name, hex code, RGB value, and HSL value. Add a section explaining when to use each method.
easy.starterCode:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>CSS Color Reference</title>
</head>
<body>
    <!-- Build a color reference page -->
</body>
</html>
easy.solution:
CSS Color Reference

A comprehensive reference for CSS color values.

Color Values Table

Preview Name Hex RGB HSL
red #FF0000 rgb(255,0,0) hsl(0,100%,50%)
blue #0000FF rgb(0,0,255) hsl(240,100%,50%)
emerald #2ECC71 rgb(46,204,113) hsl(145,63%,49%)
alizarin #E74C3C rgb(231,76,60) hsl(6,78%,57%)
peter river #3498DB rgb(52,152,219) hsl(204,70%,53%)
amethyst #9B59B6 rgb(155,89,182) hsl(282,39%,53%)
sunflower #F39C12 rgb(243,156,18) hsl(37,90%,51%)
turquoise #1ABC9C rgb(26,188,156) hsl(168,76%,42%)
wet asphalt #34495E rgb(52,73,94) hsl(210,29%,29%)
clouds #ECF0F1 rgb(236,240,241) hsl(192,20%,94%)

When to Use Each Color Method

Named Colors
Quick prototyping, basic colors (red, blue, green, etc.)
Hex Codes (#RRGGBB)
Production code, precise colors, most common format
RGB (rgb(r,g,b))
When you need programmatic color generation
RGBA (rgba(r,g,b,a))
When you need transparency on a single color
HSL (hsl(h,s%,l%))
When you need intuitive color adjustments (adjusting lightness)
HSLA (hsla(h,s%,l%,a))
Transparency with intuitive HSL adjustments

© 2026 CSS Color Reference

medium.title: Build a 'Color Picker & Contrast Checker' Page
medium.description: Create an HTML page that serves as a color tool reference. Include: a section showing all 17 basic HTML color names with colored boxes, a section demonstrating hex codes with a gradient of 10 shades of blue (from #0000FF to #ADD8E3), a section showing RGBA transparency with overlapping colored boxes, a section demonstrating HSL hue rotation (show boxes at 0°, 60°, 120°, 180°, 240°, 300°, 360°), and a section that demonstrates good vs poor contrast combinations with proper annotations.

    <!-- Build a color tools reference page -->

medium.solution:
Color Tools Reference

1. Basic HTML Color Names

The 17 standard HTML colors:

aqua black blue fuchsia gray
green lime maroon navy olive
purple red silver teal white
yellow (Total: 17 standard colors)

2. Hex Code Gradient: Blue Shades

10 shades of blue from #0000FF to #ADD8E3:

#0000FF #1E90FF #4169E1 #4682B4 #5F9EA0 #6495ED #7B68EE #87CEEB #ADD8E3 #E0F0FF

3. RGBA Transparency

Overlapping boxes showing different alpha values:

alpha 0.2
alpha 0.4
alpha 0.6

4. HSL Hue Rotation

Colors around the HSL color wheel:

0° (R) 60° (Y) 120° (G) 180° (C) 240° (B) 300° (M) 360° (R)

5. Contrast Comparison

Text ColorBackgroundSampleRatioRating
#333#FFFSample text~10:1✅ AAA
#FFF#333Sample text~10:1✅ AAA
#555#FFFSample text~5:1✅ AA
#999#FFFSample text~2.5:1❌ Fail
#CCC#FFFSample text~1.5:1❌ Fail

© 2026 Color Tools Reference

hard.title: Build a 'Color Accessibility & Theme Builder' Page
hard.description: Create a comprehensive color demonstration page that includes: a full HSL color wheel with marking of primary/secondary/tertiary colors, an RGBA layering demo showing how transparency affects color mixing, a 'color blindness simulation' section showing how different color combinations appear to users with different types of color blindness (use text annotations), a dark mode/light mode comparison section showing the same content in both color schemes, an accessibility section with contrast ratio calculations and WCAG compliance levels, and a CSS custom properties (variables) demonstration showing how colors can be managed in a theme system.
hard.starterCode:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>Color Accessibility & Theme Builder</title>
</head>
<body>
    <!-- Build a comprehensive color accessibility page -->
</body>
</html>
hard.solution:
Color Accessibility & Theme Builder

Color Wheel | RGBA Mixing | Color Blindness | Themes | Contrast | CSS Variables

HSL Color Wheel

The color wheel shows the relationship between hues at 30-degree intervals:

0° Red 120° Green 240° Blue

60° Yellow 180° Cyan 300° Magenta

30° Orange 90° Chartreuse 150° Spring 210° Azure 270° Violet 330° Rose

RGBA Transparency Mixing

When colored layers overlap with alpha transparency, they create mixed colors:

Red 40%
Green 40%
Blue 40%

The overlapping areas create new colors through additive mixing.

Color Blindness Simulation

About 8% of males and 0.5% of females have some form of color blindness. Avoid using color as the only differentiator.

Problematic: Color-only indicators

✔ Success ✘ Error

Problem: Red-green color blindness makes these indistinguishable.

Accessible: Color + Symbol

✔ Passed | ✘ Failed | ⚠ Warning | ℹ Info

Solution: Use text labels, icons, and patterns alongside color.

Test Your Colors

Use Chrome DevTools → Rendering → Emulate vision deficiencies to test your page for:

  • Protanopia — Reduced sensitivity to red light (~1% of males)
  • Deuteranopia — Reduced sensitivity to green light (~6% of males)
  • Tritanopia — Reduced sensitivity to blue light (very rare)
  • Achromatopsia — Complete color blindness (very rare)

Light Mode vs Dark Mode

Light Theme

Background: #FFFFFF | Text: #333333 | Ratio: 10:1 ✅

This is a sample paragraph in light mode. The high contrast ensures readability.

Dark Theme

Background: #1E1E1E | Text: #E0E0E0 | Ratio: ~12:1 ✅

This is a sample paragraph in dark mode. Reduced blue light can reduce eye strain.

WCAG Contrast Compliance

Level Normal Text Large Text Description
AA 4.5:1 3:1 Minimum standard (legal requirement in many jurisdictions)
AAA 7:1 4.5:1 Enhanced standard (best practice)

Sample Compliance Checks

  • #333 on #FFF 10:1 ✅ AAA
  • #FFF on #333 10:1 ✅ AAA
  • #FFF on #3498DB 4.2:1 ⚠ AA (borderline)
  • #FFF on #E74C3C 4.1:1 ⚠ AA (borderline)
  • #FFF on #F39C12 2.2:1 ❌ Fail

CSS Custom Properties for Color Management

Define a color palette using CSS variables for consistent theming:

:root {
    --primary: #3498DB;
    --secondary: #2ECC71;
    --danger: #E74C3C;
    --warning: #F39C12;
    --dark: #2C3E50;
    --light: #ECF0F1;
    --text: #333333;
    --bg: #FFFFFF;
}

[data-theme="dark"] {
    --text: #E0E0E0;
    --bg: #1E1E1E;
}

Using variables, you can switch themes by changing a single data attribute on the HTML element, and all colors update automatically.

Sample Variable-Based Theme Application

Primary color text

Secondary color text

Danger color text

Warning color text

© 2026 Color Accessibility & Theme Builder. Built for the HTML Mastery Course.
